title: School Feeding Program Coverage And Nutritional Impact Gaps In Low-Income Countries
summary: Build a verified workflow for mapping school feeding program coverage and estimating nutritional impact at sub-national scale in low-income countries, with explicit coverage-data limitations, meal-quality variation documentation, and the distinction between program existence and verified nutritional impact.
why_it_matters: WFP estimates 418 million children receive school meals globally, but coverage in low-income countries is below 20 percent — the children who most need school meals are least likely to receive them. School feeding increases enrollment by 8-12 percentage points on average, but nutritional-impact evidence is weaker and meal quality varies enormously. National coverage statistics mask district-level gaps where rural schools have no programs. Without verified sub-national coverage analysis, program expansion cannot be targeted where it would most reduce hunger and increase enrollment.

success_metrics.target: Every coverage claim documents whether data is national aggregate or sub-national, and explicitly states where district-level data is absent.
success_metrics.validation_method: Schema validation and reviewer spot-checks against coverage-data granularity documentation.

success_metrics.target: No nutritional-impact claim is merged without documenting meal-quality variation, micronutrient content, and dietary-diversity differences across programs.
success_metrics.validation_method: Reviewer confirms meal-quality and nutritional-content documentation.

success_metrics.target: Every effectiveness claim distinguishes enrollment/attendance effects (strong evidence) from nutritional-impact effects (weaker evidence).
success_metrics.validation_method: Red-team reviewer confirms enrollment-vs-nutrition evidence distinction.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, meal-quality safety review, and replication for quantitative outputs.
